name: Lion's Head Pork
recipeInstructions:
1. Coarsely dice the pork using a Chinese cleaver. Dice the water chestnuts and onion slices as well. Place all ingredients on a heavy-duty meatboard and mince with the Chinese cleaver until fully blended into a lump. This process takes about 10-15 minutes of consistent mincing.
2. Transfer the minced mixture to a bowl. Add salt, pepper, and soy sauce. Mix thoroughly and form into 4 large meatballs.
3. Heat oil in a wok over medium heat for about 1 minute. Add the meatballs and brown them on all sides for approximately 15 minutes. Reduce heat to low-medium and cook the meatballs an additional 30 minutes, lifting the cover occasionally to turn the meatballs and ensure even cooking.
4. Remove the meatballs from the wok and set aside on a platter. Add celery and cabbage to the wok. If there isn't enough leftover juice in the wok, add about 1/4 cup of water. Stir-fry for about a minute, cover, and cook for an additional 3 minutes, lifting the cover once or twice to stir the vegetables.
5. Place the cooked celery and cabbage on a serving platter and arrange the meatballs over the top.
NOTE: Celery-cabbage is a winter season vegetable. If unavailable in summer, regular cabbage can be substituted. Increase cooking time for regular cabbage to 7.5 minutes.
